title: lupinus austrohumifusus
intro: Lupinus austrohumifusus, also known as the Yellow Lupine or Yellow Bush Lupine, is a perennial plant species native to eastern Australia and Queensland. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. Lupinus austrohumifusus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is sometimes used as a substitute for the more well-known Lupinus luteus due to its similar properties.
